messages.message: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'persistRecentForgeItemIds'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

messages.message: 'isConsumable' is assigned a value but never used.

The 'selectedRangeCategories' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2230) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'selectedRangeCategories' in its own useMemo() Hook.

The 'meleeDamageTypeIds' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2750) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'meleeDamageTypeIds' in its own useMemo() Hook.

The 'rangedDamageTypeIds' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2777) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'rangedDamageTypeIds' in its own useMemo() Hook.

The 'aoeDamageTypeIds' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2802) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'aoeDamageTypeIds' in its own useMemo() Hook.

The 'attackEffectMeleeIds' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2750) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'attackEffectMeleeIds' in its own useMemo() Hook.

The 'attackEffectRangedIds' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2777) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'attackEffectRangedIds' in its own useMemo() Hook.

The 'attackEffectAoEIds' logical expression could make the dependencies of useEffect Hook (at line 2802) change on every render. To fix this, wrap the initialization of 'attackEffectAoEIds' in its own useMemo() Hook.
